objections.claim: The required digest implementation and package-boundary rule are mutually incompatible. The approach mandates runtime reuse of `canonicalize`/`sha256hex` from `merkle-dag/vendor.mjs`, while `test-boundary.mjs` must reject every non-`node:` runtime import. Importing the mandated sibling module therefore fails the boundary oracle; reimplementing it violates the frozen reuse rule. The boundary must explicitly allow exactly the sanctioned realpath-resolved `merkle-dag/vendor.mjs` import while continuing to reject `clotho/`, other package-external imports, bare packages, and undeclared dependencies.

The package-boundary oracle is not discriminating as specified. It is only run against the expected-compliant package, so a no-op `test-boundary.mjs` that always exits 0 satisfies acceptance. It needs controlled positive and branch-isolating negative fixture trees proving detection of at least: a `clotho/` import, an unauthorized package-external relative import, a bare-package import, dynamic `import()`, direct and aliased `require`/`createRequire`, prohibited code-loading escapes, and a nonempty `dependencies` field.

The affirmative decision record is required to be content-addressed while also “carrying the authority triple,” whose third member is that same decision record's id. This creates an undefined self-referential hash preimage, compounded by the absence of an exact rule stating which fields are excluded when computing a machine-record id. The decision must instead be anchored by the already-existing plan hash and `authz-N`, have its id computed by an explicitly defined sanctioned content-address procedure, and only then be referenced as the third anchor by the pending contract; it cannot include its own not-yet-derived id as an authority input.
objections.scope: candidate §5–§6 authority triple and decision-record identity
